title: [Feature] Github Release Notes
author: Soundvessel
body: ### Feature Title Add Github Release Notes ### Feature Description It looks like this repo only has release tags and isn't making actual Github releases and notes. > https://github.com/antoniandre/vue-cal > [[https://github.com/antoniandre/vue-cal/releases]()](https://github.com/antoniandre/vue-cal/releases) Traditionally you would get something like this > https://github.com/mattpocock/ts-error-translator > https://github.com/mattpocock/ts-error-translator/releases You might find [Github's ability to automatically generate realase notes](https://docs.github.com/en/repositories/releasing-projects-on-github/automatically-generated-release-notes) helpful. While there are release notes in the documentation site, Github's convention wis vastly preferred for a number of reasons outlined in Use Cases below. ### Use Case - Tools like Dependabot rely on that information to include in library update PRs in user's projects. Screenshots below of a dependabot PR on our project. - If a release fixes a vulnerability, you can link it to [security advisories](https://docs.github.com/en/repositories/releasing-projects-on-github/about-releases#about-releases) to alert downstream users - Ease of access through links to individual fixes and diffs to assess update and potential impact. - Github repo subscription alerts on new releases as well current release info on repo main page ### Additional Context - [ ] I can help implement this feature - [ ] This feature is critical to my project ### Alternative Solutions _No response_

title: [Bug] `@event-create` event does not fire, including in playground example

### Vue Cal Version

https://antoniandre.github.io/vue-cal/examples/playground

https://antoniandre.github.io/vue-cal/examples/playground

### Additional Settings

- [x] Using Composition API
- [ ] Using SSR

### Describe the Bug & Expected Behavior

`@event-create` should fire and operate per API docs

### Provide a Reproduction Link

1. Goto https://antoniandre.github.io/vue-cal/examples/playground
2. Open devtools console
3. Use page buttons to create events
4. Note lack of console logged events despite source code

Event callback is set up [here](https://github.com/antoniandre/vue-cal/blob/1c02248f3170cc0985493d0ef2953cfed6b2d34c/src/documentation/examples/playground.vue#L84) to trigger `open` method [here](https://github.com/antoniandre/vue-cal/blob/1c02248f3170cc0985493d0ef2953cfed6b2d34c/src/documentation/examples/playground.vue#L288) which should produce console log output but is does not.

title: [Bug] #cell-content` slot renders per schedule within cell but lacks identifying data
author: Soundvessel
body:
### Vue Cal Version

5.0.1-rc.40

### Vue Version

3.5.32

### Additional Settings

- [x] Using Composition API
- [ ] Using SSR

### Describe the Bug & Expected Behavior

When using schedules the `#cell-content` slot is rendered not just per cell but also per schedule (will refer as sub-cell). There is currently no returned data in this slot to identify which schedule sub-cell is rendering. Recommend returning schedule id and schedule events in slot like so:

`<template #event-content="{cell, schedule}" />` Add `schedule` property to slot props output when schedules are being used. `cell` can remain unchanged.

Output format
```js
const schedule = {
  id: 1, // schedule ID
  events: [
    { /* event */ },
    { /* event */ },
    /* etc */
  ] // reactive event data like `cell.events` but only returns the events in this schedule of cell
}
```

Schedule data returned from `#cell-content` is needed to:
1. Identify which schedule sub-cell is rendering 
2. Identify scheduled events in that sub-column
3. Use data only from events in that schedule

In our case we have a custom component that needs to tally data from that schedule sub-cell.

### Provide a Reproduction Link

N/A

title: [Bug] Online Examples of overlap prevention block valid drop and resize interactions
author: Soundvessel
body:
### Vue Cal Version

https://antoniandre.github.io/vue-cal/examples/calendar-events--interactions#ex--reject-event-dnd-or-resizing

### Vue Version

https://antoniandre.github.io/vue-cal/examples/calendar-events--interactions#ex--reject-event-dnd-or-resizing

### Additional Settings

- [ ] Using Composition API
- [ ] Using SSR

### Describe the Bug & Expected Behavior

The online ([Events Interactions](https://antoniandre.github.io/vue-cal/examples/calendar-events--interactions#ex--reject-event-dnd-or-resizing)) examples block valid drop and resize actions. Video reproduction below.

Bugs:
1. [Event Drag & Drop](https://antoniandre.github.io/vue-cal/examples/calendar-events--interactions#ex--drag-and-drop) when overlappable events is turned off events can not be dropped above or below existing events when they would fit and not overlap.
2. [Reject Event Drag & Drop or Resizing](https://antoniandre.github.io/vue-cal/examples/calendar-events--interactions#ex--reject-event-dnd-or-resizing) same as above for drops but also with resize

It follows a pattern that an existing event in a cell prevents the actions the whole cell. Day, Days, and Week views affected. Reproduction video below uses a fresh incognito window of the latest Chrome on Windows 11.

Expectation:
1. You should be able to drop and resize an event above and below another in the same cell as long as it fits.
2. I can break this into new feature request but sharing the human UX goal here might be valuable. Many users that manage schedules need to quickly move events by placing them directly above or below existing events without gaps or overlap. Rather than an overlap invalidating a user's action the option to snap it directly above or below if space allows would make such work a breeze. I have a few ideas on how this can be done that I can put into the feature request this weekend.

### Provide a Reproduction Link

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4bb86475-2be9-4dc4-ae32-18b6149a7926

title: [Feature] Ability to easily theme via utility classes e.g. Tailwind via props entry
author: Soundvessel
body:
### Feature Title

Add prop that allows you to replace or add additional classes to display with each BEM class

### Feature Description

CSS class utility libraries like Tailwind are often used for theming.

I propose vue-cal could take a reactive object prop allowing the reactive application of  utility classes to vue-cal's internal structures. The existing BEM class names are a good point of reference and could double as object properties for this prop; used to map the utility classes to where/when they need to reactively output.

For example:

```vue
<template>
  <vue-cal :classMap="classMap" />
</template>

<script setup>
import { computed } from 'vue'
const { someProp } = defineProps()

const classMap = computed(() => ({
  'vuecal__header': someProps.value ? 'bg-stone-200 dark:bg-stone-700' : 'bg-blue-200 dark:bg-blue-700',
  'vuecal__view-button': 'text-sm text-stone-900 dark:text-stone-100 font-normal',
  'vuecal__view-button--active': 'font-bold',
}))
</setup>
```

The tricky part is some of the logic could result in utility class clashing like in the case here where `vuecal__view-button` with and without `vuecal__view-button--active` can result in both `font-normal` and `font-bold` applied in the active case.

This could be solved specifically for Tailwind by providing an optional prop to add the `twMerge` function as long as merging order is consistent (maybe by how properties are ordered in config object?).

Or modifiers could be applied as reactive data properties to the html as they are easy use in tailwind; e.g. `<div class="vuecal__view-button vuecal__view-button--active" data-active />` Tailwind can then use `data-active:font-bold` etc

Or we could leave the BEM modifier classes out of the config and instead expose logic by allowing us to map functions. This is similar to passThrough with PrimeVue's UI library.

So instead of our config object having this conflict:
```js
{
  'vuecal__view-button': 'text-sm text-stone-900 dark:text-stone-100 font-normal',
  'vuecal__view-button--active': 'font-bold',
}
```

It could be something like:
```js
{
  // vue can combine classes in arrays etc
  'vuecal__view-button': props => [ 
    'text-sm text-stone-900 dark:text-stone-100',
    props.active ? 'font-bold' : 'font-normal',
  ],
}
```

Instead of BEM names more simply component names could be used... lots of different ways to approach this. So more input from users and maintainers might be good here.






### Use Case

While currently we can workaround using Tailwind's `@apply` syntax in CSS to override the BEM classes, it isn't reactive to custom props without extra steps like creating custom variants etc. Use of `@apply` is also discouraged and it also fragments our theming which is normally applied to components directly.

A reactive prop to apply CSS classes of our choosing would allow much more flexibility consistent with how we are already using these utility class libraries directly with other UI components.


### Additional Context

- [ ] I can help implement this feature
- [ ] This feature is critical to my project

### Alternative Solutions

_No response_

title: [Feature] Use new Temporal API with polyfill instead of Date and custom prototypes to avoid many pitfalls
author: Soundvessel
body:
### Feature Title

Replace use of Date and custom prototype methods with Temporal API standard via temporal-polyfill

### Feature Description

Temporal is the upcoming browser native replacement for soon to be depreciated Date objects. It solves Date's many pain points and potential inaccuracies when dealing with calculations of historical data affected by timezone changes etc.

Many browsers [already support the new standard](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Temporal#browser_compatibility) but it can be reliably be used in libraries like yours today via [temporal-polyfill](https://github.com/fullcalendar/temporal-polyfill/).

### Use Case

## Why?

> Issues with Date Objects
> - No support for time zones other than the user’s local time and UTC
> - Parser behavior so unreliable it is unusable
> - Date object is mutable
> - DST behavior is unpredictable
> - Computation APIs are unwieldy
> - No support for non-Gregorian calendars
> 
> Temporal fixes these problems by:
> - Providing easy-to-use APIs for date and time computations
> - First-class support for all time zones, including DST-safe arithmetic
> - Dealing only with objects representing fixed dates and times
> - Parsing a strictly specified string format
> - Supporting non-Gregorian calendars
>
> _https://tc39.es/proposal-temporal/docs/_

It would also replace the need for the custom prototypes:

> ```js
> const start = Temporal.PlainDateTime.from("2021-01-01T12:34:56");
> const end = start.add({
>   years: 1,
>   months: 2,
>   weeks: 3,
>   days: 4,
>   hours: 5,
>   minutes: 6,
>   seconds: 7,
>   milliseconds: 8,
> });
> console.log(end.toString()); // 2022-03-26T17:41:03.008
> ```
>
> _https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Temporal/PlainDateTime/add#adding_a_duration_

And it would greatly help your upcoming time zone support by providing standards compliant and accurate calculations even when dealing with historical data affected by new/changing timezones.

### Lightweight Polyfill

[temporal-polyfill](https://github.com/fullcalendar/temporal-polyfill) is recommended as it is more lightweight than [@js-temporal/polyfill](https://github.com/js-temporal/temporal-polyfill) by avoiding BigInt operations and therefore reliance on JSBI.

To provide full standards support in browsers yet to adopt Temporal the polyfill global updates the `Intl` native object to accept various Temporal objects like `Temporal.PlainDateTime`. It also adds the convince 'toTemporalInstant' method to 'Date'. [temporal-polyfill/global source](https://github.com/fullcalendar/temporal-polyfill/blob/main/packages/temporal-polyfill/src/global.ts).

### Note on Output Format Performance

> The toLocaleString() method of [Temporal.PlainDateTime](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Temporal/PlainDateTime) instances returns a string with a language-sensitive representation of this date-time. In implementations with [Intl.DateTimeFormat API](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Intl/DateTimeFormat) support, this method delegates to Intl.DateTimeFormat.
> 
> **Every time toLocaleString is called, it has to perform a search in a big database of localization strings, which is potentially inefficient. When the method is called many times with the same arguments, it is better to create an [Intl.DateTimeFormat](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Intl/DateTimeFormat) object and use its [format()](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Intl/DateTimeFormat/format) method**, because a DateTimeFormat object remembers the arguments passed to it and may decide to cache a slice of the database, so future format calls can search for localization strings within a more constrained context.
>
> _https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Temporal/PlainDateTime/toLocaleString_

For example we would want to cache the expensive lookup like this instead of repeatedly calling ` new Intl.DateTimeFormat` with the same config:

```js
// some format that is used repeatedly
const timeShortFormat = new Intl.DateTimeFormat(
  'en-US',
  {
    hour: 'numeric',
    minute: '2-digit',
    hour12: true,
  },
)

// now the expensive lookup is cached and more performant when used repeatedly for output
console.log(timeShortFormat.format(SomeTemporalPlainTime))
```

### Additional References
- [Schedule-X v3: Embracing the Future with Temporal API](https://schedule-x.dev/blog/schedule-x-v3-temporal-api) (blog post about a similar library that made the switch)
- [Temporal API MDN Docs](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Temporal)
- [tc39 Temporal Cookbook](https://tc39.es/proposal-temporal/docs/cookbook.html)

Our current project like many stores datetime in ISO UTC strings for everything, e.g. `2025-02-23T07:00:00.000Z`, (although UNIX epoch timestamps are also very reliable too). That way we can let the client handle timezone adjustments for display either with detected or provided timezone using Temporal etc. The UI framework we use expects Date in a few places for inputs so we just convert in and out at those boundaries in order to keep the data type/format as consistent as possible throughout our app.

### Additional Context

- [ ] I can help implement this feature
- [ ] This feature is critical to my project

### Alternative Solutions

_No response_

title: [Feature] Time zone support for calendar
author: vishnu-exerp
body:
### Feature Title

Time zone support for calendar

### Feature Description

We need to specify the timezone which the calendar to be rendered.
In our project we manage some clubs that are located in different timezones and we need to show bookings at that timezone and especially the current time (now line) line need to be showing the correct time even if the browser time is in another timezone.

The event start and end time can stay the same and and need to be aligned with the provided timezone(ie, the date and time needs to be in the timezone that is provided, we can only manage the calendar date, this way the old ones will work exactly same even if they upgrade without providing timezone.)
if the timezone is not provided we can take browser timezone.

### Use Case

If i have a club at USA and i'm working from france in browser, the now line and the current time is based on france in the vue calendar now.
We need an option to display everything at the provided timezone, and needs the current time to be shown according to the timezone provided.

### Additional Context

- [ ] I can help implement this feature
- [ ] This feature is critical to my project

### Alternative Solutions

_No response_

title: [Feature] Adjust calendar size properly for mobile screens
author: kennyhei
body: ### Feature Title Make calendar automatically fit to mobile screens ### Feature Description Hi, thanks for this library, very easy to use and looks good out of the box on bigger screens. Currently the calendar is not responsive enough for mobile screens and everything looks pretty crammed: Here's example how Google Calendar looks on mobile: When comparing, mostly the header needs some more work in vue-cal (buttons overflowing, texts overlapping etc.) For top buttons, maybe some kind of carousel implementation could work? Example: - "Week" is active: [...y] [Days] **[Week]** [Month] [Ye...] - "Month" is active: [...ys] [Week] **[Month]** [Years] [Ye...] Just an idea. ### Use Case I have app that's mostly used in mobile screens so it's important that calendar also works well in mobile and would make this library more tempting overall to other devs as well. ### Additional Context - [ ] I can help implement this feature - [ ] This feature is critical to my project ### Alternative Solutions _No response_

Adding scroll in schedules

### Feature Description

If there is more schedules than the calendar can contain make the container scrollable with the titles aligning to the columns.

Some times the schedules can be more than the limited numbers unlike the day, week or month view.

### Use Case

If i have the view for the staffs at my office and schedules for the day it will be difficult to allocate everything if the staff count is more than 10(just an example) or more.

title: [Feature] Add support for horizontal schedules
author: j-osephlong
body:
### Feature Title

Add support for horizontal schedules

### Feature Description

Currently Schedules are displayed vertically only, which on a week view can limit horizontal space for event items, and if there exists many schedules, make items unreadable. It would be great if schedules could be made to render horizontally, with the header being the left most column in the week view, and each schedule being its own row.

Edit (2025/12/15 12:14PM): I should add that my use case **disables time**, and so I'm envisioning this column taking the place of the time column. If the time column was enabled, I suppose that the first column would be the schedule, and the time span in the time column would be repeated per schedule, which certainly complicates things.

### Use Case

A schedule for employees, where the events being scheduled are planned projects or to-dos, and by dropping the item at some x,y on the calendar, both a time and employee are selected for the event. In my case, there could be anywhere from 4 to 10 employees that need to be visible at any time on the calendar.

### Additional Context

- [x] I can help implement this feature
- [x] This feature is critical to my project

### Alternative Solutions

I don't believe that there is any way to implement this with this package as of now. If I am mistaken please let me know!

title: [Feature] Add number field to provide week number in week view
author: dmitry-sazonov-official
body:
### Feature Title

Add number field to provide week number in week view

### Feature Description

Problem

In week view, vue-cal provides start and end dates for each week, but:
1. There is no direct way to access the ISO week number for the currently displayed week.
2. The #title slot shows only string of dates with the week number, so users cannot display the week number in a custom format.

Many apps require week numbers in the UI (for business calendars, ERP/CRM integration, or reporting) and want to control how the title is rendered, combining start/end dates with the week number.

Proposal
- Add a new computed field (or property) such as numberOfWeek.
- Allow usage in the #title slot so developers can format text freely

### Use Case

The user wants to render the week title in the following format:
`Week 44 (October 2025)`

This gives full flexibility to customize the title while still providing the week number programmatically.

### Additional Context

- [x] I can help implement this feature
- [x] This feature is critical to my project

### Alternative Solutions

_No response_

title: [Feature] TypeScript: incorrect type of emit @event-create, not documented return of emit
author: ChillMouse
body:
### Feature Title

TypeScript: incorrect type of emit @event-create, not documented return of emit

### Feature Description

https://github.com/antoniandre/vue-cal/discussions/14#discussioncomment-14694098

Not documented correct return of emit @event-create, and incorrect type for emit

### Use Case

If you typed the return value of emit in your code, the IDE will not suggest that the resolve function exists

Interface of emit 'event-create' is: `'event-create': (value: VueCalEventEvents & VueCalCellEvents) => any`

type VueCalEventEvents haven't the function `resolve`

title: [Bug] Event resizing does not work in the production build
author: Gleb-Iv
body:
### Vue Cal Version

5.0.1-rc.33

### Vue Version

3.5.21

### Additional Settings

- [x] Using Composition API
- [ ] Using SSR

### Describe the Bug & Expected Behavior

In the production build, the event resizing feature is not working.
It works correctly in development mode

You can reproduce the issue on the example page: [https://antoniandre.github.io/vue-cal/examples/calendar-events--interactions](https://github.com/antoniandre/vue-cal/issues/url)

The problem starts from version 5.0.1-rc.30 and persists in 5.0.1-rc.33.

The console shows the following error:
Uncaught (in promise) TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'props')



### Provide a Reproduction Link

1. Go to [https://antoniandre.github.io/vue-cal/examples/calendar-events--interactions](https://github.com/antoniandre/vue-cal/issues/url)
2. Create an event in the calendar
3. Start resizing the event

title: [Bug] types in 5.0.1-rc.33
author: may-day
body:
### Vue Cal Version

5.0.1-rc.33

### Vue Version

3.5.22

### Additional Settings

- [x] Using Composition API
- [ ] Using SSR

### Describe the Bug & Expected Behavior

Hi, thanks for adding types in rc33. 
I have problems using them as they are now mentioned in package.json. If i use them like so:
```typescript
import {VueCal, type VueCalView} from 'vue-cal'
```
this shows an error:
```
Could not find a declaration file for module 'vue-cal'. '/..../node_modules/vue-cal/dist/vue-cal.es.js' implicitly has an 'any' type.
There are types at '/.../node_modules/vue-cal/dist/vue-cal.d.ts', 
but this result could not be resolved when 
respecting package.json "exports". 
The 'vue-cal' library may need to update its package.json 
or typings.ts-plugin(7016)
```



### Provide a Reproduction Link

Modifying vue-cal's package.json by adding the `types` entry to the exports object would do the trick:
```json
"exports": {
    ".": {
      "import": "./dist/vue-cal.es.js",
      "require": "./dist/vue-cal.cjs.js",
      "types": "./dist/vue-cal.d.ts"
    },
 
```

Am i missing something here?

title: [Bug] Slot resizing is not working on macOS in any browser
author: Gleb-Iv
body:
### Vue Cal Version

>5.0.1-rc.29

### Vue Version

3.5.21

### Additional Settings

- [x] Using Composition API
- [ ] Using SSR

### Describe the Bug & Expected Behavior

Resizing the slot doesn’t work on macOS in any browser.
You can reproduce the issue on the example page: [https://antoniandre.github.io/vue-cal/examples/calendar-events--interactions](url)

The problem starts from version 5.0.1-rc.29 and persists in 5.0.1-rc.33.

The console shows the following error:
Uncaught (in promise) TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'props')

### Provide a Reproduction Link

on macOS: 
1. Go to [https://antoniandre.github.io/vue-cal/examples/calendar-events--interactions](url)
2. Create an event in the calendar
3. Start resizing the event
